key_insight: A microstructure-aware convolutional deep network can translate atomistic shock physics into continuum-scale predictions, enabling end-to-end shock-to-detonation modeling.
engineering_relevance: This approach offers a parameter-free way to predict run-to-detonation behavior in energetic composites, which could improve safety, design, and performance assessment.
method: Train MISTnetX on large-scale molecular dynamics simulations of shock propagation through complex microstructures.
Use the network to learn shock-microstructure interactions, hotspot formation, and transition-to-deflagration behavior.
Couple MISTnetX outputs as sub-grid information inside continuum finite-element simulations of mechanics, heat transport, shocks, and chemistry.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to a synthetic yet realistic nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composite, where the framework predicts the full run-to-detonation transition.
watch_item: The reported validation is on a synthetic but realistic composite, so generalization to other energetic materials and manufacturing-defect populations may require additional testing.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
